zh.lifeSummary: 當James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston於1276年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾登東納爾德時，他的父親Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies31歲，母親Lady Margaret de Bonkyl27歲。他和Isabel Graham至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1333年7月19日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德享年57歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭斯可迪夏 波德斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston于1276年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾登東納爾德时，他的父亲Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies31岁，母亲Lady Margaret de Bonkyl27岁。他和Isabel Graham至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1333年7月19日在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德享年57岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰斯可迪夏 波德斯。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston was born in 1276, in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, was 31 and his mother, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, was 27. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Isabel Graham. He died on 19 July 1333, in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57, and was buried in Scottish Borders, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston est né en 1276, à Dundonald, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Isabel Graham. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1333, à Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Scottish Borders, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston 1276 in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies 31 und seine Mutter Lady Margaret de Bonkyl 27. Er hatte mit Isabel Graham mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 19. Juli 1333 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Scottish Borders, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston è nato il nel 1276 nel Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Isabel Graham. Morì il 19 luglio 1333, nel Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Scottish Borders, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: James Stewart 1st Baron Pearstonが1276年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアダンドナルドで生まれた時，父Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garliesは31歳，母Lady Margaret de Bonkylは27歳であった。彼はIsabel Grahamとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1333年7月19日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドバーウィク-ウプオン-トウィードで57歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドスコッティッシュ ボルダースにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston(이)가 1276년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 던도널드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret de Bonkyl(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Isabel Graham(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1333년 7월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 버윅-업-트위드에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 스콧이시 볼더스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston nasceu em 1276, em Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Isabel Graham. Ele faleceu em 19 de julho de 1333, em Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Scottish Borders, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston родился в 1276 г. в местности дундональд, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, 27. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Isabel Graham. Он умер 19 июля 1333 г. в местности бирвикк-упон-твид, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности скоттиш бордирс, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Stewart 1st Baron Pearston nació en 1276, en Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Isabel Graham. Murió el 19 de julio de 1333, en Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Scottish Borders, Escocia, Reino Unido.
